posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
React Dev:

I want to set a few columns to a specific value every day. It’s a like a daily checklist that I want to reset so my team can update their tasks as the day goes by. How can I do that easily? I may want to change it every day or every week, depending on the board

Hey @reactdev441 !

You can do this with a recurring automation.

A common setup is using a scheduled automation like:
“When date arrives every day > set Status to X” or “clear column.”

For a daily reset, some teams add a Date column (for example “Reset date”) and use an automation such as:
“When date arrives > set Status to ‘Not started’.”

Then add another automation to move that date forward (for example, push the date by 1 day) so it keeps repeating.

If you want it weekly instead, you can use the same idea but push the date by 7 days instead of 1.

This approach lets the board reset automatically without needing someone to update each item manually. If the reset should apply to many items, just make sure they all share the same reset date column so the automation triggers consistently.

If you’d like hands-on help or want us to walk through this live, you can book a 1:1 paid 60-minute strategy session with our team here:
Calendly

posts.author: MsquareAutomation (Msquare Automation Solutions)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @reactdev441
You can do this easily using Make Automation

Set up a scheduled scenario that runs every day (or week) at a specific time. The scenario will:

  • Find the items on your board

  • Update the selected columns to the default values you want based on the conditions (e.g., reset statuses, numbers, checkboxes, etc.)

This works perfectly for daily or weekly checklists, since everything resets automatically without manual effort.
